quizzes.title: General Knowledge
quizzes.description: Test your overall awareness with standard general knowledge questions.

questions.question: Capital of India?
questions.explanation: Delhi is the capital of India.

questions.question: Largest planet?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.

questions.question: Fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: Cheetah is the fastest land animal, reaching speeds of over 100 km/h.

questions.question: Largest ocean?
questions.explanation: Pacific is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth.

questions.question: Currency of Japan?
questions.explanation: Japan uses the Yen as its currency.

questions.question: Mount Everest is in?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest lies on the border between Nepal and China (Tibet).

questions.question: How many continents?
questions.explanation: There are seven continents: Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe, and Australia.

questions.question: H2O is?
questions.explanation: H2O is the chemical formula for water.

questions.question: National animal of India?
questions.explanation: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.question: Sun rises in?
questions.explanation: Earth rotates from west to east, so the Sun appears to rise in the East.

quizzes.title: React.js Basics
quizzes.description: Test your fundamentals on React components, hooks, virtual DOM, and properties.

questions.question: React is maintained by?
questions.explanation: Meta (formerly Facebook) maintains React.

questions.question: Hook for state?
questions.explanation: useState manages local state inside functional components.

questions.question: JSX stands for?
questions.explanation: JSX means JavaScript XML; it lets us write HTML-like syntax inside JavaScript.

questions.question: Default React file extension?
questions.explanation: JSX is the standard extension for files containing React markup.

questions.question: Navigation library?
questions.explanation: React Router is the standard navigation and routing library for React.

questions.question: Virtual DOM purpose?
questions.explanation: It optimizes rendering by updating only elements that changed in DOM.

questions.question: State should be?
questions.explanation: Treat state as immutable; use setter functions to trigger re-renders.

questions.question: Hook for side effects?
questions.explanation: useEffect runs side effects (fetching data, listening to events) in functional components.

questions.question: Props are?
questions.explanation: Props are read-only and passed down from parent to child.

questions.question: Build tool?
questions.explanation: Vite is a modern frontend build tool and dev server.

quizzes.title: Movies & Entertainment
quizzes.description: Show off your cinema and pop-culture trivia knowledge.

questions.question: Iron Man belongs to?
questions.explanation: Iron Man is a prominent character in Marvel Comics and the MCU.

questions.question: Avatar director?
questions.explanation: James Cameron directed the groundbreaking sci-fi film Avatar.

questions.question: Harry Potter author?
questions.explanation: J.K. Rowling is the creator of the Harry Potter book series.

questions.question: Sherlock Holmes creator?
questions.explanation: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle created the famous detective Sherlock Holmes.

questions.question: Oscar awards are for?
questions.explanation: Academy Awards (Oscars) recognize achievements in the movie industry.

questions.question: Elsa appears in?
questions.explanation: Elsa is a main character in Disney's Frozen franchise.

questions.question: Batman city?
questions.explanation: Batman protects the fictional Gotham City.

questions.question: Hogwarts teaches?
questions.explanation: Hogwarts is a school of witchcraft and wizardry.

questions.question: Minions first appeared in?
questions.explanation: Minions debuted in the 2010 animated film Despicable Me.

questions.question: Joker is villain of?
questions.explanation: The Joker is Batman's archenemy.

quizzes.title: Science
quizzes.description: Challenge yourself with questions covering chemistry, physics, biology, and space.

questions.question: Symbol of Gold?
questions.explanation: Au (from Latin aurum) is the chemical symbol for Gold.

questions.question: Nearest star?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to the Earth.

questions.question: Human heart chambers?
questions.explanation: The human heart has 4 chambers: two atria and two ventricles.

questions.question: Gas plants absorb?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb carbon dioxide (CO2) during photosynthesis.

questions.question: Speed of light unit?
questions.explanation: The speed of light is measured in meters per second (m/s), approx. 3 x 10^8 m/s.

questions.question: DNA full form starts with?
questions.explanation: DNA stands for Deoxyribonucleic Acid.

questions.question: Boiling point water?
questions.explanation: At sea level, water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (100°C).

questions.question: Planet known as Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet due to iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

questions.question: Largest organ?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ of the human body.

questions.question: Electric current unit?
questions.explanation: Ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current.

quizzes.title: Sports
quizzes.description: Test your memory and statistics across popular global sports.

questions.question: Players in football team?
questions.explanation: A standard football match consists of 11 players per team on the pitch.

questions.question: Olympics every?
questions.explanation: The Olympic Games are held every 4 years.

questions.question: Cricket bat made of?
questions.explanation: Professional cricket bats are made from English willow wood.

questions.question: NBA sport?
questions.explanation: NBA stands for National Basketball Association.

questions.question: Tennis Grand Slam in UK?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is the prestigious Grand Slam tournament held in London, UK.

questions.question: Chess pieces each side?
questions.explanation: Each player starts a chess game with 16 pieces.

questions.question: Badminton shuttle called?
questions.explanation: The badminton shuttlecock is commonly referred to as a birdie.

questions.question: Kabaddi players/team?
questions.explanation: There are 7 active players on the court for each team in Kabaddi.

questions.question: FIFA World Cup sport?
questions.explanation: FIFA organizes the football (soccer) World Cup.

questions.question: Highest runs format?
questions.explanation: Test cricket is the longest format where teams play over 5 days, resulting in the highest number of runs.
